---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=IBM+Plex+Mono:wght@400;600&family=IBM+Plex+Sans:ital,wght@0,300;0,500;0,700;1,300&display=swap" rel="stylesheet"/>
    theme: none
    css: []
    toc: false
    sidebar: false
    margin-left: "0"
    margin-right: "0"
---

```{=html}
<style>
*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

:root {
  --red:       #c0392b;
  --red-light: #e74c3c;
  --cream:     #f5f0e8;
  --off-white: #ede8df;
  --dark:      #1a1612;
  --mid:       #3d3530;
  --muted:     #7a6f68;
  --accent:    #d4a853;
  --sidebar-w: 220px;
}

/* Kill Quarto chrome */
#quarto-header, .quarto-title-banner, #title-block-header,
.navbar, .sidebar, #quarto-sidebar, #quarto-margin-sidebar { display: none !important; }

body, html {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif !important;
  background: var(--cream);
  color: var(--dark);
  overflow-x: hidden;
}
main.content, div#quarto-content, .page-columns {
  padding: 0 !important; margin: 0 !important;
  max-width: 100% !important; width: 100% !important; gap: 0 !important;
}

/* ── TOP NAV ── */
.sg-nav {
  position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 9999;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;
  padding: 0 3rem; height: 60px;
  background: var(--dark);
  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--red);
}
.sg-nav-logo {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.85rem;
  color: var(--cream); letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase;
  text-decoration: none;
}
.sg-nav-logo span { color: var(--red-light); }
.sg-nav-links { display: flex; gap: 2.5rem; }
.sg-nav-links a {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--muted); text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s;
}
.sg-nav-links a:hover, .sg-nav-links a.active { color: var(--red-light); }

/* ── PAGE LAYOUT ── */
.proposal-layout {
  display: flex;
  min-height: 100vh;
  padding-top: 60px;
}

/* ── LEFT SIDEBAR ── */
.proposal-sidebar {
  width: var(--sidebar-w);
  min-width: var(--sidebar-w);
  background: var(--dark);
  position: fixed;
  top: 60px; left: 0; bottom: 0;
  overflow-y: auto;
  display: flex; flex-direction: column;
  border-right: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
  z-index: 100;
}

.sidebar-logo {
  padding: 2rem 1.5rem 1.5rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
  display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem;
}
.sidebar-logo-icon {
  width: 64px; height: 64px;
  background: var(--red);
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.7rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  color: var(--cream); text-align: center; line-height: 1.4;
}
.sidebar-logo-links { display: flex; gap: 1.25rem; }
.sidebar-logo-links a {
  color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.9rem; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s;
}
.sidebar-logo-links a:hover { color: var(--cream); }

.sidebar-search {
  padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08);
}
.sidebar-search input {
  width: 100%;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
  color: var(--cream);
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem;
  padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s;
}
.sidebar-search input::placeholder { color: var(--muted); }
.sidebar-search input:focus { border-color: var(--red); }

.sidebar-nav { padding: 0.75rem 0; flex: 1; }
.sidebar-nav a {
  display: block;
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--muted);
  padding: 0.6rem 1.5rem;
  text-decoration: none;
  border-left: 3px solid transparent;
  transition: color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s, background 0.2s;
  line-height: 1.4;
}
.sidebar-nav a:hover { color: var(--cream); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04); }
.sidebar-nav a.active {
  color: var(--cream);
  border-left-color: var(--red);
  background: rgba(192,57,43,0.1);
}
.sidebar-nav a.sub {
  padding-left: 2.5rem;
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  color: var(--muted);
}
.sidebar-nav a.sub:hover { color: var(--cream); }
.sidebar-nav a.sub.active {
  color: var(--cream);
  border-left-color: var(--red);
  background: rgba(192,57,43,0.08);
}

/* ── MAIN CONTENT ── */
.proposal-main {
  margin-left: var(--sidebar-w);
  flex: 1;
  padding: 4rem 5rem 6rem;
  max-width: 900px;
}

.proposal-page-title {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif !important;
  font-size: clamp(1.8rem, 3vw, 2.6rem) !important;
  font-weight: 700 !important; color: var(--dark) !important;
  line-height: 1.1 !important;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem !important; border: none !important; padding: 0 !important;
}
.proposal-author-label {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.65rem; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--muted); margin-bottom: 0.2rem; margin-top: 1.5rem;
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem;
}
.proposal-author-label::before { content:''; display:block; width:20px; height:1px; background:var(--red); }
.proposal-author {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--mid); margin-bottom: 2.5rem;
}
.proposal-divider { border: none; border-top: 1px solid #d8d2c8; margin: 2.5rem 0; }

.proposal-section { scroll-margin-top: 70px; margin-bottom: 3.5rem; }
.proposal-section h2 {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif !important;
  font-size: 1.5rem !important; font-weight: 700 !important; color: var(--dark) !important;
  border-left: 4px solid var(--red) !important; padding-left: 1rem !important;
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem !important; margin-top: 0 !important;
}
.proposal-section h3 {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif !important;
  font-size: 1.05rem !important; font-weight: 600 !important; color: var(--mid) !important;
  margin-top: 1.5rem !important; margin-bottom: 0.6rem !important;
}
.proposal-section h4 {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif !important;
  font-size: 0.95rem !important; font-weight: 600 !important; color: var(--mid) !important;
  margin-top: 1.2rem !important; margin-bottom: 0.4rem !important;
  font-style: italic;
}
.proposal-section p {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.975rem; font-weight: 300; line-height: 1.9;
  color: var(--mid); margin-bottom: 1.1rem;
}
.proposal-section p strong { font-weight: 600; color: var(--dark); }
.proposal-section a { color: var(--red); text-decoration: none; }
.proposal-section a:hover { color: var(--red-light); text-decoration: underline; }

.info-box {
  background: var(--off-white); border-left: 3px solid var(--red);
  padding: 1rem 1.25rem; margin: 1.5rem 0;
  font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 300; line-height: 1.7; color: var(--mid);
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif;
}
.info-box strong { font-weight: 600; color: var(--dark); }

.proposal-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1.5rem 0; font-size: 0.875rem; }
.proposal-table thead { background: var(--dark); }
.proposal-table thead th {
  padding: 0.65rem 1rem;
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.68rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase;
  font-weight: 600; color: var(--cream); border: none; text-align: left;
}
.proposal-table tbody td {
  padding: 0.6rem 1rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #d8d2c8;
  font-weight: 300; color: var(--mid); font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif;
}
.proposal-table tbody tr:hover { background: var(--off-white); }

.tag-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.5rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
.chip {
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.68rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  padding: 0.25rem 0.65rem; background: var(--off-white);
  border: 1px solid #d0c8be; color: var(--mid);
}
.chip.red { background: rgba(192,57,43,0.1); border-color: rgba(192,57,43,0.3); color: var(--red); }

/* Timeline table */
.timeline-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1.5rem 0; font-size: 0.875rem; }
.timeline-table thead { background: var(--dark); }
.timeline-table thead th {
  padding: 0.65rem 1rem;
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 0.68rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase;
  font-weight: 600; color: var(--cream); border: none; text-align: left;
}
.timeline-table tbody td {
  padding: 0.6rem 1rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #d8d2c8;
  font-weight: 300; color: var(--mid); font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif;
}
.timeline-table tbody tr:hover { background: var(--off-white); }
.timeline-table .bar { color: var(--red); font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -1px; }

@media (max-width: 860px) {
  .proposal-sidebar { display: none; }
  .proposal-main { margin-left: 0; padding: 2rem 1.5rem 4rem; }
  .sg-nav { padding: 0 1.5rem; }
}

div.cell { margin: 1.5rem 0; }
div.sourceCode {
  background: var(--dark) !important;
  border: none !important;
  border-left: 3px solid var(--red) !important;
  border-radius: 0 !important;
  padding: 0 !important;
  overflow-x: auto;
}
div.sourceCode pre {
  margin: 0 !important;
  padding: 1rem 1.25rem !important;
  background: transparent !important;
  font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace !important;
  font-size: 0.82rem !important;
  line-height: 1.6 !important;
  color: #f5f0e8 !important;
}
div.cell-output-display {
  margin-top: 1rem; border: 1px solid #d8d2c8;
  padding: 0.5rem; background: white;
}
</style>

<!-- ── TOP NAV ── -->
<nav class="sg-nav">
  <a href="index.html" class="sg-nav-logo">SG <span>CPI</span> Forecast</a>
  <div class="sg-nav-links">
    <a href="index.html#home">Home</a>
    <a href="index.html#about">About</a>
    <a href="index.html#projects">RShiny App</a>
    <a href="proposal.html" class="active">Proposal</a>
    <a href="poster.html">Poster</a>
  </div>
</nav>

<div class="proposal-layout">

  <!-- ── SIDEBAR ── -->
  <aside class="proposal-sidebar">
    <div class="sidebar-logo">
      <div class="sidebar-logo-icon">SG<br>CPI</div>
      <div class="sidebar-logo-links">
        <a href="index.html" title="Home">🏠</a>
        <a href="https://github.com/kevinyap24/ISSS608-Project_G1" title="GitHub" target="_blank">⌥</a>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="sidebar-search">
      <input type="text" placeholder="Search…" id="sidebar-search-input" />
    </div>

    <nav class="sidebar-nav" id="sidebar-nav">
      <a href="#overview" class="active">1 · Overview &amp; Objectives</a>
      <a href="#data">2 · Data</a>
      <a href="#methodology">3 · Methodology</a>
      <a href="#methodology-eda" class="sub">3.1 · Data Preparation &amp; EDA</a>
      <a href="#methodology-decomposition" class="sub">3.2 · Decomposition</a>
      <a href="#methodology-forecasting" class="sub">3.3 · Forecasting</a>
      <a href="#prototype">4 · Prototype</a>
      <a href="#minutes">5 · Minutes of Meeting</a>
      <a href="#timeline">6 · Timeline &amp; Allocation</a>
    </nav>
  </aside>

  <!-- ── MAIN CONTENT ── -->
  <main class="proposal-main">

    <h1 class="proposal-page-title">Forecasting Singapore's Consumer Price Index</h1>
    <div class="proposal-author-label">Kevin A. Suyapto &nbsp;·&nbsp; Lee Jangho &nbsp;·&nbsp; Pan Yiqiong</div>
    <div class="proposal-author">ISS608 Project — Group 1 &nbsp;·&nbsp; 15 Mar, 2026</div>
    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════
         1. OVERVIEW AND OBJECTIVES
    ══════════════════════════════════════════ -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="overview">
      <h2>1 · Overview and Objectives</h2>

      <h3>What is CPI?</h3>
      <p>
        <strong>Consumer Price Index (CPI)</strong> is a widely used economic indicator that measures
        inflation by tracking changes in the price level of a basket of goods and services consumed
        by households. CPI provides an important reference for economic policy, wage adjustments, and
        cost of living assessments. In Singapore, CPI statistics are compiled and published by the
        Singapore Department of Statistics and are structured into 10 expenditure categories such as
        food, housing, transport, healthcare, and recreation.
      </p>
      <p>
        The CPI dataset naturally forms a hierarchical structure, where detailed consumption items
        aggregate into broader categories and ultimately contribute to the overall inflation index.
        For example, individual items such as rice, meat, and vegetables belong to lower level
        sub-categories, which aggregate into food-related groups and eventually into the overall CPI.
      </p>

      <h3>Why CPI?</h3>
      <p>
        Inflation directly affects the purchasing power of households, cost of doing business, and
        calibration of monetary policies. For an economy like Singapore, where natural resources are
        a scarcity, price levels are sensitive to the global commodities cycle, exchange rates, and
        disruptions in supply chain activities.
      </p>
      <p>
        <strong>For households:</strong> The rise in inflation erodes purchasing power, making the
        price of everyday essentials such as food, transport, and utilities more expensive. CPI
        forecasts help households plan ahead — adjusting monthly budgets, timing big-ticket
        purchases, and planning savings and investments.
      </p>
      <p>
        <strong>For businesses:</strong> Pricing strategies and cost planning are tightly related to
        expected inflation of direct materials. Access to reliable CPI forecasts allows businesses to
        anticipate pressures in the cost of doing business tied to inflation.
      </p>
      <p>
        <strong>For policy makers:</strong> Unlike central banks in most countries, monetary policy
        managed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) is centered on managing the Singapore
        Dollar against a trade-weighted basket of currencies (S$NEER). A stronger S$NEER makes
        imports cheaper, directly dampening consumer prices. CPI forecasts therefore complement
        policy-making by helping assess whether inflationary pressures are temporary or structural.
      </p>

      <h3>Project Objective</h3>
      <div class="info-box">
        <strong>Objective:</strong> To evaluate and compare ETS, ARIMA, Auto-ARIMA + XGBoost, and
        Prophet time-series models and to deploy the best-performing model for each CPI index in an
        interactive R Shiny application.
      </div>

      <h3>Scope of Analysis</h3>
      <p>
        This study examines monthly Singapore CPI data from <strong>January 2020 to December 2025</strong>,
        covering multiple CPI categories from the overall index to detailed expenditure components
        (Base Year: 2024 = 100), sourced from the CEIC database via SMU Library.
      </p>
      <p>
        The chosen period captures a particularly turbulent inflation cycle. Beginning just before
        the COVID-19 pandemic, the dataset spans the Circuit Breaker disruption of 2020, the global
        supply chain constraints and demand surge of 2021–2022, and the subsequent disinflation into
        2024–2025. This offers a basis for studying how inflation dynamics evolved across different
        expenditure categories under rapidly shifting economic conditions.
      </p>
      <p>
        By combining exploratory visualisation, time series decomposition, and forecasting, this
        study aims to characterise the behaviour of inflation across CPI components and generate
        near-term forecasts for future price movements.
      </p>
      <div class="tag-row">
        <span class="chip red">Headline CPI</span>
        <span class="chip">Core CPI</span>
        <span class="chip">Food &amp; Beverages</span>
        <span class="chip">Housing &amp; Utilities</span>
        <span class="chip">Transport</span>
        <span class="chip">Healthcare</span>
        <span class="chip">Education</span>
        <span class="chip">Clothing &amp; Footwear</span>
      </div>
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════
         2. DATA
    ══════════════════════════════════════════ -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="data">
      <h2>2 · Data</h2>

      <h3>Data Source</h3>
      <p>
        The dataset is obtained from the <strong>CEIC Database</strong>, accessible via the SMU
        Library. It contains monthly Singapore CPI data with base year 2024, spanning from January
        1961 to January 2026, last updated on 23 February 2026. For this project, the analysis
        period is restricted to <strong>January 2020 – January 2025</strong> to focus on the
        post-pandemic inflation dynamics.
      </p>
      <p>
        Since this project focuses on time series analysis, the original CPI dataset in wide format
        was transformed into a long format to make it suitable for time series modelling and analysis.
      </p>

      <h3>CPI Hierarchy</h3>
      <p>
        The dataset preserves the full hierarchical structure of the official Singapore CPI
        classification published by the <strong>Department of Statistics (DOS)</strong>. The
        hierarchy spans four levels:
      </p>
      <table class="proposal-table">
        <thead>
          <tr><th>Level</th><th>Description</th><th>Example</th></tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td>0</td><td>Top Level (Overall Index)</td><td>Consumer Price Index (CPI)</td></tr>
          <tr><td>1</td><td>Major Group</td><td>Food, Transport, Health</td></tr>
          <tr><td>2</td><td>Sub-Category</td><td>Food Excl FBSS, Accommodation</td></tr>
          <tr><td>3</td><td>Detailed Class (Leaf Node)</td><td>Rice &amp; Cereals, Fast Food</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>

      <h3>CPI Basket Weights</h3>
      <p>
        Forecasts are reconciled using official expenditure weights from the
        <a href="resources/cpijan26.pdf" target="_blank">Singapore Consumer Price Index, Jan 2026</a>.
        The ten major groups and their weights (out of 10,000) are:
      </p>
      <table class="proposal-table">
        <thead>
          <tr><th>Major Group</th><th>Weight</th></tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td>Food</td><td>2,042</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Housing &amp; Utilities</td><td>2,938</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Transport</td><td>1,307</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Health</td><td>1,008</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Education</td><td>579</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Recreation, Sport &amp; Culture</td><td>595</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Household Durables &amp; Services</td><td>547</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Miscellaneous Goods &amp; Services</td><td>438</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Information &amp; Communication</td><td>381</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Clothing &amp; Footwear</td><td>165</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════
         3. METHODOLOGY
    ══════════════════════════════════════════ -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="methodology">
      <h2>3 · Methodology</h2>
      <p>
        The analytical workflow consists of three stages executed in sequence: Data Preparation
        and EDA, Time Series Decomposition, and Time Series Forecasting. Each stage is described
        in its own subsection below.
      </p>
    </section>

    <!-- 3.1 DATA PREPARATION AND EDA -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="methodology-eda">
      <h2>3.1 · Data Preparation and EDA</h2>

      <h3>Data Wrangling</h3>
      <p>
        The dataset was obtained from the <strong>CEIC database via the SMU Library</strong> and
        contains monthly Consumer Price Index (CPI) data from January 1961 to January 2026, with
        2024 = 100 as the base year. The data was imported into R using <code>read_excel()</code>
        from the <strong>readxl</strong> package. The dataset was initially structured in a
        <strong>wide format</strong>, where rows represent dates and columns represent CPI categories
        and subcategories.
      </p>
      <p>
        To focus on recent inflation dynamics, the dataset was filtered to include the period
        <strong>January 2020 to December 2025</strong> using <code>mutate()</code>,
        <code>as_date()</code>, and <code>filter()</code> from the <strong>dplyr</strong> and
        <strong>lubridate</strong> packages. The dataset was then reshaped into a
        <strong>tidy long format</strong> using <code>pivot_longer()</code> from <strong>tidyr</strong>,
        allowing each CPI category to be treated as an individual time series.
      </p>
      <p>
        Given the hierarchical structure of CPI categories, labels were generated using
        <code>mutate()</code> and <code>str_detect()</code> from the <strong>stringr</strong>
        package to distinguish between different levels of CPI aggregation. Column names containing
        long hierarchical strings were also standardised using functions from <strong>stringr</strong>
        and <strong>dplyr</strong> to improve readability. Data integrity checks were performed using
        <code>sum(is.na())</code> to ensure that no missing values or misaligned entries were present.
        The cleaned dataset was then saved using <code>write_rds()</code> for reproducibility. An
        interactive data table was also created using the <strong>DT</strong> package to enable
        sorting, filtering, pagination, and export options.
      </p>

      <h3>Exploratory Data Analysis</h3>
      <p>
        Exploratory data analysis was conducted to understand the <strong>trend, seasonality, and
        temporal dependence</strong> of CPI series. Trend patterns are visualised using
        <code>plot_time_series()</code> from the <strong>timetk</strong> package and
        <strong>ggplot2</strong> for customised plotting. Seasonal behaviour was examined using
        <code>plot_seasonal_diagnostics()</code> from <strong>timetk</strong> to detect monthly,
        quarterly, and yearly patterns. Temporal dependence was further analysed using
        <code>plot_acf_diagnostics()</code> to generate <strong>ACF, PACF, and CCF plots</strong>,
        which help assess lag relationships and inform time series modelling.
      </p>
      <p>
        To support <strong>Shiny dashboard development</strong>, reusable wrapper functions were
        created for each EDA component. These functions expose key parameters, allowing flexible
        selection of CPI series, hierarchical levels, and visualisation options. This design improves
        interactivity, reproducibility, and scalability when exploring CPI dynamics across categories.
      </p>
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- 3.2 TIME SERIES DECOMPOSITION -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="methodology-decomposition">
      <h2>3.2 · Time Series Decomposition</h2>

      <h3>STL Decomposition</h3>
      <p>
        Seasonal and Trend decomposition using Loess (<strong>STL</strong>) is applied to each CPI
        series. STL is widely used for economic time series because it is robust to outliers and
        flexible enough to handle changing seasonal patterns. This is particularly relevant for
        Singapore CPI data, which experienced structural disruptions during the COVID-19 pandemic
        and the subsequent inflation cycle.
      </p>
      <p>
        Each CPI variable is converted into a monthly time series beginning in <strong>January
        2020</strong>, with a frequency of <strong>12 observations per year</strong>. The STL
        algorithm then decomposes the series into <strong>trend</strong>, <strong>seasonal</strong>,
        and <strong>remainder</strong> components using locally weighted regression (Loess). This
        approach allows the seasonal component to evolve gradually over time rather than assuming a
        fixed seasonal structure.
      </p>

      <h3>Observed Series Summary</h3>
      <p>
        To provide an overview of the observed CPI series, summary statistics are computed for each
        CPI category. These include the <strong>starting value in January 2020</strong>, the
        <strong>ending value in December 2025</strong>, the <strong>absolute increase</strong>, and
        the <strong>percentage change</strong> over the study period. These summary measures provide
        a quick comparison of inflation dynamics across different CPI components.
      </p>

      <h3>Decomposition Interpretation</h3>
      <p>
        The magnitude of each component (trend, seasonal, and remainder) is quantified and
        normalised by the mean of the corresponding CPI series. This normalisation allows the
        relative importance of each component to be compared across different CPI categories. Series
        with a dominant trend component indicate persistent inflation movements, while stronger
        seasonal components suggest regular cyclical price behaviour. Larger remainder components
        may indicate irregular shocks or volatility not explained by structural patterns.
      </p>
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- 3.3 TIME SERIES FORECASTING -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="methodology-forecasting">
      <h2>3.3 · Time Series Forecasting</h2>
      <p>
        Four forecasting approaches are implemented and benchmarked using the
        <strong>modeltime</strong> framework in R. All models are trained on data prior to
        February 2025 and evaluated on a held-out test set of 12 months using a nested forecasting
        scheme. The best-performing model for each CPI series is selected based on the lowest RMSE
        and refitted on the full dataset before generating future forecasts.
      </p>

      <h3>Model Suite</h3>
      <table class="proposal-table">
        <thead>
          <tr><th>Model</th><th>Type</th><th>Key Strength</th></tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td>ETS</td><td>Classical</td><td>Captures trend and seasonality via exponential smoothing</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Auto-ARIMA</td><td>Classical</td><td>Automated order selection for optimal fit on stationary series</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Auto-ARIMA + XGBoost</td><td>Hybrid</td><td>Combines Auto-ARIMA with XGBoost to capture non-linear residuals</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Prophet</td><td>Additive Regression</td><td>Robust to missing data and handles seasonal shifts flexibly</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>

      <h3>Hierarchical Forecasting</h3>
      <p>
        Forecasts are generated at the most granular level (leaf nodes) and aggregated upward using
        a <strong>bottom-up reconciliation</strong> approach. Each leaf node forecast is weighted
        according to its official CPI basket weight, and parent-level indices are derived as
        weighted averages of their child components.
      </p>

      <h3>Evaluation</h3>
      <p>
        Models are compared using RMSE, MAE, and MASE. The best-performing model per series is
        identified by the lowest RMSE on the 12-month hold-out test set, refitted on the full
        dataset, and used to generate forward-looking forecasts.
      </p>
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════
         4. PROTOTYPE
    ══════════════════════════════════════════ -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="prototype">
      <h2>4 · Prototype</h2>
      <p>The following wireframes illustrate the planned layout of the R Shiny application.</p>

      <h3>Landing Page</h3>
      <img src="images/Landing_page.jpg"
           alt="RShiny App Landing Page Prototype"
           style="width:100%; border: 1px solid #d8d2c8; margin-top: 1rem;" />

      <h3>EDA Page</h3>
      <img src="images/EDA.jpg"
           alt="RShiny App EDA Page Prototype"
           style="width:100%; border: 1px solid #d8d2c8; margin-top: 1rem;" />

      <h3>Data Table</h3>
      <img src="images/datatable.jpg"
           alt="RShiny App Data Table Prototype"
           style="width:100%; border: 1px solid #d8d2c8; margin-top: 1rem;" />

      <h3>Forecasting Page</h3>
      <img src="images/ForecastSketch.png"
           alt="RShiny App Forecasting Page Prototype"
           style="width:100%; border: 1px solid #d8d2c8; margin-top: 1rem;" />
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════
         5. MINUTES OF MEETING
    ══════════════════════════════════════════ -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="minutes">
      <h2>5 · Minutes of Meeting</h2>

      <h3>Meeting 1 — Project Kickoff</h3>
      <p><em>Date: 2 March 2026 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Attendees: All group members</em></p>
      <p>The team aligned on the project scope to forecast Singapore CPI and the level of
      granularity using CEIC data. Initial task allocation was agreed upon: Exploratory Data
      Analysis, ACF, and PACF analysis (Yiqiong), Time Series Decomposition (Jangho), Forecasting
      (Kevin). <a href="minutes/Meeting%20Minutes%201.pdf" target="_blank">View minutes →</a></p>

      <h3>Meeting 2 — Progress Discussion</h3>
      <p><em>Date: 11 March 2026 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Attendees: All group members</em></p>
      <p>Each team member presented updates based on the tasks assigned in the previous meeting.
      <a href="minutes/Meeting%20Minutes%202.pdf" target="_blank">View minutes →</a></p>

      <h3>Meeting 3 — Poster Preparation</h3>
      <p><em>Date: 1 April 2026 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Format: Online (Teams) &nbsp;·&nbsp; Attendees: All group members</em></p>
      <p>
        The team reviewed professor feedback on the proposal, noting gaps around the tidymodels
        framework, nested forecasting explanation, and holdout methodology. The teamagreed to
        address all points in the final application. The meeting then focused on the project
        poster: Jangho prepared the skeleton and template, Kevin verified the A1/300 dpi
        specification, and the group aligned on sections (Motivations, Methodology, EDA,
        Decomposition, Forecasting, Conclusion &amp; Future Work). The team agreed to prioritise
        visuals over text, include an RMSE comparison table, and submit the finalised poster
        by 1 April midnight.
        <a href="minutes/Meeting%20Minutes%203.pdf" target="_blank">View minutes →</a>
      </p>
      
       <h3>Meeting 4 — Shiny App Development</h3>
      <p><em>Date: 1 April 2026 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Format: Online (Teams) &nbsp;·&nbsp; Attendees: All group members</em></p>
      <p>
        Following poster submission, the team shifted focus to Shiny app integration. Kevin
        presented the project website and forecast prototype, Yiqiong shared the updated EDA
        module, and Jangho contributed the decomposition components. Key issues identified
        included an overly red colour theme (flagged by Yiqiong), a gap between actual and
        forecast lines in the forecast plot (flagged by Jangho), and inconsistent control panel
        styling (flagged by Kevin). The team agreed to continue refinements in parallel, targeting
        a completed prototype by 3 April and final Shiny app submission by 5 April midnight.
        <a href="minutes/Meeting%20Minutes%204.pdf" target="_blank">View minutes →</a>
      </p>
    </section>

    <hr class="proposal-divider" />

    <!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════
         6. PROJECT TIMELINE AND WORK ALLOCATION
    ══════════════════════════════════════════ -->
    <section class="proposal-section" id="timeline">
      <h2>6 · Project Timeline and Work Allocation</h2>
      <p>
        The project is divided into several stages covering data preparation, exploratory analysis,
        time series modelling, and application development. Tasks are distributed among team members
        according to their assigned analytical modules.
      </p>

      <h3>Work Allocation</h3>
      <table class="proposal-table">
        <thead>
          <tr><th>Task</th><th>Team Member</th></tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td>Data Preparation &amp; Exploratory Data Analysis</td><td>Pan Yiqiong</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Time Series Decomposition</td><td>Lee Jangho</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Time Series Forecasting</td><td>Kevin A. Suyapto</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Shiny Application Development</td><td>All Members</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Final Report &amp; Presentation</td><td>All Members</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>

      <h3>Project Timeline</h3>
      <table class="timeline-table">
        <thead>
          <tr><th>Task</th><th>Week 1</th><th>Week 2</th><th>Week 3</th><th>Week 4</th></tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr>
            <td>Project Setup &amp; Data Collection</td>
            <td class="bar">██</td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>Exploratory Data Analysis</td>
            <td class="bar">██</td><td class="bar">██</td><td></td><td></td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>Time Series Decomposition</td>
            <td></td><td class="bar">██</td><td></td><td></td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>Forecasting Model Development</td>
            <td></td><td></td><td class="bar">██</td><td></td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>Shiny App Development</td>
            <td></td><td></td><td class="bar">██</td><td class="bar">██</td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>Final Report &amp; Presentation</td>
            <td></td><td></td><td></td><td class="bar">██</td>
          </tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </section>

  </main>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  var sections  = ['overview','data','methodology','methodology-eda',
                   'methodology-decomposition','methodology-forecasting',
                   'prototype','minutes','timeline'];
  var sideLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.sidebar-nav a');

  function updateActive() {
    var scrollY = window.scrollY + 100;
    var current = sections[0];
    sections.forEach(function(id) {
      var el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (el && el.offsetTop <= scrollY) current = id;
    });
    sideLinks.forEach(function(a) {
      a.classList.toggle('active', a.getAttribute('href') === '#' + current);
    });
  }
  window.addEventListener('scroll', updateActive, { passive: true });
  updateActive();

  sideLinks.forEach(function(a) {
    a.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
      var id = a.getAttribute('href').replace('#','');
      var el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (el) {
        e.preventDefault();
        window.scrollTo({ top: el.offsetTop - 70, behavior: 'smooth' });
      }
    });
  });

  document.getElementById('sidebar-search-input').addEventListener('input', function() {
    var q = this.value.toLowerCase();
    sideLinks.forEach(function(a) {
      a.style.display = a.textContent.toLowerCase().includes(q) ? '' : 'none';
    });
  });
})();
</script>
```
